geography: TX — Hill Country (Hays/Travis-exurban/Comal/Kerr/Bandera/Blanco) + Houston exurbs (Montgomery/Liberty/Brazoria/Fort Bend west/Waller) + DFW exurbs (Parker/Ellis/Johnson/Hood/Wise) + East TX piney-woods (Smith/Gregg/Angelina/Nacogdoches/Bowie)
thesis: LOW-PE-attention vertical. Only Wind River Environmental + Walters Environmental have done meaningful TX rollup. TCEQ chapter 285 aerobic maintenance contracts mandate recurring revenue every 4 months. Aging boomer owners + non-prestigious trade = succession-vacuum opportunity. +2 baseline L4 modifier for low competition.

sources_worked.note: Live About / Team page fetches on top spine candidates yielded owner identity + tenure + successor verification on 14 high-priority businesses

sources_worked.source: WebSearch for verified TX septic businesses by region
sources_worked.note: 4-region parallel search (Hill Country / Houston exurbs / DFW exurbs / East TX) yielded the full 80-row spine; all spine entries have at least one verified source URL
Cross-corroborated owner identity for Premier Septic Solutions ($4M rev disclosed), Cisco Septic, Total Septic, Larry's Septic

sources_partial.issue: 404 — member directory not publicly accessible; only general TX Online Wastewater Assn membership tiers visible. Workaround: web search for TOWA-member companies (returned name-matches from articles + cross-refs)

sources_partial.source: County OSSF Maintenance Provider PDF lists
sources_partial.url: Hood County 2024 + Guadalupe County 2-13-25 + Coryell County 2022 + Austin County 8.2019
sources_partial.issue: PDFs returned as binary FlateDecode-compressed streams via WebFetch — text not extractable in this environment. Worked around via name-match cross-references in web search snippets (e.g., Coleman Aerobic, Double R Services in Hood County, JB Septic Systems in Guadalupe County)

sources_blocked.error: HTTP 404 on direct WebFetch attempt (ColdFusion .cfm UI requires session state)
sources_blocked.fallback_used: Web search + verified business websites + county PDF lists + cross-corroborated entity names

Homepage fetches + web search owner cross-refs for those rows where blocked; per skill rule, the rows where live team-page fetch was BLOCKED are capped at C_watch (Hill Country Wastewater Services, Ledford Services)

sources_blocked.error: PDFs not parseable as plain text via WebFetch (binary FlateDecode compressed)
sources_blocked.fallback_used: Web search cross-references to surface specific company names (Coleman Aerobic, Double R Services, JB Septic Systems)

passed_candidates: Carnes Enterprise (Pipe Creek/Bandera) — 11-county Hill Country aerobic, Jim Carnes ~65 35-yr solo, spouse-only-office confirmed via live-fetch
Taylor Septic Services (Decatur/Wise) — Charles ~85 (62-yr pastoring career disclosed), 37 yr husband-wife, no 2nd-gen via live-fetch
Weaver's Septic Service (Rusk/Cherokee) — MP #0291 original-issuance TCEQ license, Jerry Emerson NOT a Weaver = DBA bought-the-brand, solo via live-fetch
Superior Septic (Round Rock/Williamson) — Ray McEachern 43-yr sole owner via live-fetch, TCEQ #20500 + 20+ TX municipality route, porta-toilet caveat for verification
Environmental Septic Services LLC (Ovilla/Ellis) — Lori 29-yr owner via live-fetch, Ellis County contracted MP = code-mandated B2B moat (STRONGEST L2 in cohort)
demoted_to_b_with_reasons: Meiners Septic & Aerobic (Conroe/Montgomery) — MC00001 FIRST TX MP license, 31 yrs, owner identity NOT disclosed on About page (anonymized) + online booking present (partial modernization offset) = B_forward pending owner ID surfacing. Strong A-rerate candidate.

supabase_write.note: All 14 fully-scored businesses persisted via direct INSERT via Supabase MCP execute_sql (no septic-specific RPC built yet; followed inline pattern of prior verticals). Remaining 66 spine rows (C_watch / D_pass via light-touch scoring) are in local JSON only as documented in septic_targets.json under remaining_businesses_summary — not persisted because the fully-scored subset is the actionable cohort.
